skill1.kitLine: Activates after landing 50 normal attack(s). Affects the target(s). Deals 471.86% of final ATK as additional damage.
skill1.note: Verbatim from prior hand-authored slot. Values match kit Lv10.

skill1.kitLine: Activates only when in Annihilation State status. Affects 2 enemy unit(s) nearest to the crosshair every 10 shot(s). Deals 15.62% of final ATK as damage.
skill1.note: Annihilation-State gate (9s of 40s cycle) approximated as steady-state every-44-hits proxy. 2-target collapsed to 1 (single boss). Both approximations documented in caveats (⚑4/⚑5).

skill1.kitLine: Anti A.T. Field: Damage Taken ▲ 0.83% for 30 sec, stacks up to 30 time(s).
skill1.note: Modeled at 30-stack cap (24.9%) as near-permanent boss debuff. Note flags this as 'slightly generous' — reaching 30 stacks assumes ~300 shots inside the 9s window (⚑4/⚑5).

skill2.kitLine: Activates when entering Full Burst while in Annihilation State status. Affects self. Attack Damage ▲ 30.97% for 10 sec.
skill2.where: skill2[0]: trigger burstCast → self, buff attackDamagePct 30.97, 10s
skill2.note: Trigger is burstCast (not fullBurstEnter) because Annihilation State exists only via HER OWN burst — correctly prevents over-credit in multi-B3 teams. Sub-second timing shift (cast vs FB-entry) negligible. Documented in caveat.

skill2.kitLine: Effect 1: MG heating up speed ▼ 100% for 3 sec.
skill2.note: Ambiguous localization (⚑2): reading A = wind-up frozen (self-nerf, implausible), reading B = instant full fire rate for 3s (~+107 rounds, plausible). No engine vocabulary for wind-up ladder either way. Measure before modeling.
No ammo-dump vocabulary in engine. Damage content (forced early reload) approximated by the reloadSpeedPct window in skill2[1]. The dump itself is ~timing-neutral (she refills to 300 either way).

skill2.kitLine: Effect 3: Constantly recovers 3.77% of the skill user's final Max HP every 1 sec over 3 sec.
skill2.where: skill2[1]: heal event (collapsed 3 ticks → 1 event at cast)
skill2.note: Really activates ~9s AFTER burst cast (at Annihilation State end); schema has no delayed trigger so event fires 9s early. Recovery-consumer teammates (e.g. Crown) key off this. Documented in caveat (⚑3).

skill2.kitLine: Effect 4: Reload speed is fixed at a 60% increase for 1 rounds.
skill2.note: Window sized so buff covers ~ONE reload per burst cycle (mag cycle ≈ 9.6s; buffed reload ≈ 1.27s). 'Fixed at' may mean non-composing but no other reload buff in kit, so equivalent solo. Timing shifted 9s early (same caveat as Effect 3, ⚑3).

burst.kitLine: Effect 1: Normal Attack Damage Multiplier ▼ 40% for 9 sec.
Effect 3: ATK ▲ 46.8% of the skill user's ATK for 9 sec.
burst.note: Caster-relative ATK buff. Verbatim from prior hand-authored slot.

burst.kitLine: Annihilation: After Annihilation State ends, fires powerful attacks at targets affected by Anti A.T. Field. Deals 6.62% of final ATK as additional damage. Mirrors the stack count of Anti A.T. Field for certain targets.
burst.note: Modeled as one hit at burst cast. Real hit lands ~9s later INSIDE the Full Burst window — may be FB-boosted in-game (finding F2). Stack count baked to 30 cap. Both approximations documented in caveats.

burst.kitLine: Anti A.T. Field status is removed after the effect is triggered.
burst.note: SILENT DROP. The debuff removal on Annihilation fire (~t+9s) is represented nowhere — not in blocks, config, or unmodeled. The 30s duration on the skill1[1] debuff provides a natural lapse, but real removal at t+9s would reduce debuff uptime by ~21s per 40s cycle. The note's 'slightly generous' / 'rebuilds each cycle' language partially acknowledges debuff over-credit but does not address this specific removal mechanic. Direction: HOT (over-credits damageTakenPct uptime).

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: cadence tuple (MG wind-up ladder / 300 ammo / reloadFrames 161) is the unverified datamine (⚑1)
documentedConfounds.why: Direction unknown — if real MG fire rate or reload differs from datamine, all hit-count proxies and shot economies shift. Magnitude could be large but sign is unpredictable without footage.

documentedConfounds.text: skill1: the in-Annihilation-State every-10-shots proc + Anti A.T. Field stacks are steady-state proxies (every 44 hits, debuff at its 30-stack cap) (⚑4/⚑5)
documentedConfounds.why: Both the 44-hit proxy and the 30-stack-cap assumption are generous. If real stacks < 30, the finisher (198.6%) and the debuff (24.9%) are over-credited. The note explicitly says 'slightly generous' and 'may be optimistic'.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: the Full-Burst-entry Attack Damage buff is encoded as burstCast because it requires Annihilation State (granted only by HER OWN burst)
documentedConfounds.why: Correctness fix, not a gap. Identical to fullBurstEnter in single-B3 comps; correctly prevents over-credit in multi-B3 teams. No damage distortion.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: Emergency Repair really activates ~9s AFTER burst cast; heal event + reload-speed window start at cast (⚑3)
documentedConfounds.why: Heal is inert without a recovery-consumer teammate. Reload-speed window starting 9s early shifts which reload is buffed but net effect (one fast reload per cycle) is approximately preserved. Small timing distortion, no clear directional bias.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: 'MG heating up speed ▼ 100% for 3 sec' and 'Removes 100% of ammo' are UNMODELED (⚑2)
documentedConfounds.why: If reading B is correct (instant full fire rate for 3s post-reload), she gains ~+107 free rounds per burst cycle that the sim does not credit — a COLD bias. The ammo dump is ~timing-neutral per the note. Net direction likely COLD if the plausible reading holds.

documentedConfounds.text: burst: the Annihilation finisher (6.62% × stacks) is modeled as one 198.6% hit at cast; the real hit lands ~9s later INSIDE the Full Burst window, so it may be FB-boosted in-game (F2)
documentedConfounds.why: If the finisher is FB-boosted in-game, the sim under-credits it by the Full Burst damage multiplier (~10-20%). 198.6% ATK is a significant hit; the FB boost on it is non-trivial. COLD direction.

documentedConfounds.text: MISSING: 'Anti A.T. Field status is removed after the effect is triggered' — debuff removal at ~t+9s is not modeled; 30s duration provides natural lapse but over-credits uptime by ~21s per 40s cycle
documentedConfounds.why: The damageTakenPct debuff (up to 24.9%) persists for 30s in the sim but should be removed at ~t+9s when Annihilation fires. This over-credits the debuff's damage amplification for ~52% of each cycle. Partially masked by the note's 'slightly generous' flag on stack count, but the removal mechanic itself is a silent drop.

authorIntentSummary: The override is a parser-baseline hypothesis (never fielded, no board data) with hand-authored skill1 and burst slots preserved from a prior analysis, plus an authored skill2 from the 2026-07-16 kit-parse. Skill1 models the 50-hit proc verbatim and approximates the Annihilation-State-gated every-10-shots proc as a steady-state every-44-hits proxy with the Anti A.T. Field debuff baked to its 30-stack cap. Skill2 correctly re-triggers the FB-entry buff on burstCast (not fullBurstEnter) and models Emergency Repair's heal + reload-speed as a single event at cast (9s early). Burst encodes the four Annihilation State self-buffs verbatim and collapses the Annihilation finisher to one 198.6% hit at cast. Key known-not-yet-faithful spots: the MG wind-up/ammo-dump mechanics are unmodeled (ambiguous localization, no engine vocabulary), the finisher timing shift may cost a FB boost, the debuff removal on Annihilation fire is silently dropped, and the entire cadence tuple is unverified datamine.
